1 hour ago

--

The JavaScript ecosystem is evolving faster than ever. AI-assisted development is changing how software is written. Front-end architectures continue to grow more complex. Teams are balancing modern frameworks with legacy applications while trying to deliver better user experiences at an increasingly rapid pace.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

For developers, architects, and engineering leaders, staying current is no longer optional-it’s essential.
That’s why we’re excited to announce that registration is now open for JS Days 2026, a free two-day virtual JavaScript conference taking place on September 16–17, 2026.
Designed for JavaScript developers, React engineers, software architects, and technical leaders, JS Days brings together experienced practitioners to share practical insights, production lessons, and real-world engineering strategies-not marketing presentations or theoretical discussions.
Whether you’re building enterprise-scale applications, modernizing existing platforms, or exploring AI-powered development, JS Days 2026 is designed to help you make better technical decisions.
Why JS Days 2026 Matters
Technology conferences often introduce the latest frameworks and tools, but few spend enough time discussing what happens after adoption-when applications need to scale, teams grow, technical debt accumulates, and long-term maintainability becomes the real challenge.
JS Days 2026 focuses on those conversations.
Across two days of expert-led sessions, attendees will explore how experienced engineering teams are approaching architecture, AI integration, UI development, and enterprise application design in today’s rapidly changing JavaScript landscape.
The objective is simple: deliver ideas that developers can evaluate, discuss, and apply immediately within their own projects.
Learn From Engineers Solving Real-World Problems
Every session at JS Days 2026 is delivered by professionals actively building and maintaining production software.
Rather than focusing on abstract concepts, speakers will share practical approaches, implementation decisions, lessons learned, and architectural trade-offs from real projects.
Key topics include:

• AI-assisted JavaScript and React development for production environments

• Designing modular frontend architectures that remain maintainable over time

• Integrating React, Ext JS, and legacy systems without disrupting existing applications

• Enterprise UI design patterns that improve usability and scalability

• Real-world application case studies, including a complete recruitment platform walkthrough

• Low-code development strategies that accelerate delivery while maintaining quality
Whether you’re writing code every day or making architectural decisions for your organization, these sessions are designed to provide practical value long after the conference ends.
Meet an Experienced Speaker Lineup
JS Days 2026 features a growing roster of experienced JavaScript professionals and product experts who work directly with enterprise development teams.
Confirmed speakers include:

• James Cahill, General Manager at Sencha — Opening keynote on the future of enterprise JavaScript development

• Marc Gusmano, Sales Engineer at Sencha — Building AI agents using JavaScript, React, and ReExt

• Wemerson Januario, Developer Advocate at Sencha

• César Martell, Software Developer — A production case study showcasing a real recruitment application

• Andres Villalba, Sales Engineer at Sencha

• Rafael Méndez, Sencha MVP — Enterprise UI design best practices
Additional speakers will be announced as the event approaches.
Every presentation concludes with live Q&A, giving attendees an opportunity to explore implementation challenges, architectural decisions, and practical trade-offs directly with the speakers.
A Conference Built Around Busy Development Teams
Professional development shouldn’t require taking an entire week away from work.
JS Days 2026 has been designed with working developers in mind. Sessions are delivered in focused 45-minute formats that provide meaningful technical depth while fitting naturally into a typical workday.
Day One — September 16
The conference opens with the keynote before moving into technical sessions covering JavaScript architecture, AI-assisted development, frontend engineering, and enterprise application design.
Day Two — September 17
The second day expands into React development, enterprise UI design, low-code workflows, interactive discussions, and live community Q&A.
Attend the sessions most relevant to your work-or join both days for the complete experience.
A Truly Accessible Developer Conference
Attending industry conferences often means navigating travel budgets, approval processes, accommodation costs, and scheduling challenges.
JS Days removes those barriers entirely.
The conference is:

• Completely free to attend

• Fully virtual

• Accessible from anywhere in the world

• Open to individual developers and entire engineering teams
Many organizations use virtual conferences as shared learning opportunities-watching sessions together, discussing key takeaways, and identifying ideas worth applying across current projects.
Practical Content You Can Put to Work
The best technical conferences don’t simply introduce new technologies-they help developers make better decisions.
Throughout JS Days 2026, speakers will explore questions many engineering teams are already facing:

• How do JavaScript applications continue to scale without accumulating unnecessary technical debt?

• Where does AI genuinely improve software development, and where should teams remain cautious?

• What architectural patterns support long-term maintainability across multiple frameworks?

• Which development practices consistently translate from prototypes into production systems?
Attendees should leave with practical strategies, fresh perspectives, and actionable ideas they can bring back to their teams-not just another collection of presentation slides.
Who Should Attend?
JS Days 2026 is designed for professionals building modern web applications, including:

• JavaScript developers

• React, Angular, and Vue developers

• Front-end engineers

• Software architects

• Engineering managers and technical leads

• Teams responsible for enterprise application development
If your work involves building, scaling, or modernizing JavaScript applications, you’ll find sessions relevant to your day-to-day challenges.
Register in Under a Minute
Getting started is simple.
Visit jsdays.io, complete the short registration form, and you’ll receive:

• Speaker announcements

• Agenda updates

• Session reminders

• Event access information
There are no registration fees, no approval process, no waiting list, and no credit card required.
Event Details
JS Days 2026
Dates: September 16–17, 2026
Format: Free virtual JavaScript conference
Location: Online
Cost: Free
If you’re looking for practical insights, experienced speakers, and meaningful conversations about the future of JavaScript development, now is the perfect time to reserve your place.
Register today and join thousands of developers, architects, and engineering leaders from around the world for two days of learning, discussion, and community.
JS Days 2026 is organized by Sencha, part of Idera, Inc., creators of Ext JS, ReExt, and GXT. For decades, Sencha has helped organizations build scalable, data-intensive, enterprise-grade JavaScript applications used across financial services, healthcare, manufacturing, government, and other industries. Today, Sencha technologies are trusted by more than 2 million developers and 150,000+ enterprises worldwide, including many Fortune 500 organizations.
